title: Tactics: Discovered Attacks
summary: Master the art of discovered attacks - one of the most powerful tactical weapons in chess that can win material or deliver checkmate.
description: A discovered attack occurs when a piece moves away to reveal an attack from another piece behind it. This is a fundamental tactical pattern that appears in every level of chess. GM-level players use discovered attacks to create double attacks, win material, and deliver checkmate.
objectives: Recognize positions where a discovered attack is possible
Understand the difference between discovered attacks and discovered checks
Learn to create discovered attacks from seemingly static positions
Master the concept of the 'sacrificial' discovered attack
plans: Look for pieces lined up with enemy pieces - the piece behind might be able to attack
Consider moving a blocking piece to reveal an attack from your other piece
Discovered attacks often work best when combined with other threats
commonMistakes: Don't move a piece to create a discovered attack if it exposes your own king
Always calculate what the opponent can do after your discovered attack
A discovered attack is often just the first move - be ready for the follow-up
completionTitle: Powerful tactical weapon in your arsenal
completionSummary: You've learned how to create and exploit discovered attacks, one of the most devastating tactical patterns in chess.

steps.title: Setup for discovered attack
steps.instruction: Play e4 to begin the game and set up tactical possibilities.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black responds
steps.instruction: Black plays e5, mirroring White's central pawn.
steps.coaching: Symmetrical positions can still contain tactical opportunities.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3 to develop toward the center.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black develops the knight to c6, attacking e5.

steps.title: The bishop lines up
steps.instruction: Move Bc4, placing the bishop on a square where it could attack f7.
steps.coaching: The bishop is now aimed at f7. If something moves off the e5-f6 diagonal, we could have a discovered attack.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop moves to c5, putting pressure on f2.

steps.title: Create the discovered attack
steps.instruction: Play d4! This moves the queen off the d1-h5 diagonal, but more importantly, we're setting up a discovered attack on the e5 knight.
steps.coaching: After d4, the knight on e5 is pinned - it cannot move because it would expose the king on e8 to a discovered attack from the bishop on c4!

steps.title: Black must capture
steps.instruction: Black is forced to capture because moving the knight would expose the king.
steps.coaching: This is the power of the discovered attack - it creates forced moves.

steps.title: Win a piece
steps.instruction: Capture on d4 with the knight, winning a piece.
steps.coaching: The knight captures, developing and winning material. The discovered attack forced Black's hand.
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the knight.
